dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, respond to a residence in Richmond for an elderly fall.
Hello, I'm Rosa, an EMT. This is my partner, Jim. Can you tell me what's happening?
I'm Mark, her son. I found her on the floor, unresponsive to commands and altered. She's 93.
Ma'am, tell me your name.
She's alert but not oriented to person or place.
Airway is patent. She's not maintaining posture; we kept her in a supine position.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Rate and depth of respirations
dialogue.utterance: Respiration rate is 20 per minute, regular.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Oxygen saturation measured with pulse oximetry
dialogue.utterance: Oxygen saturation is at 99%. Clear bilateral breath sounds.

dialogue.micro_intent: check circulation - Pulse rate and quality
dialogue.utterance: Pulse rate is 76, regular and strong.
Blood pressure is 185/70.
Skin is warm and dry.
Any known allergies or medications she takes regularly?
She has a history of vertigo and hypertension. Some confusion, but not this severe.
She was last seen normal last night at 10 p.m.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: I found her on the floor inside the house. I don't know how long she's been there.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Pupils are equal and round, reactive to light.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Gross visual examination
dialogue.utterance: No deformities or signs of injury in head or neck. No DCAPBTLS.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam - Drainage; EMT
dialogue.utterance: Nasal passages and oral cavity are clear.

dialogue.micro_intent: thorax/lungs/cardio exam - Equal chest rise and fall
dialogue.utterance: Chest rises and falls equally. No asymmetry.
Abdomen is soft, non-tender. No masses noted.

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Peripheral pulses
dialogue.utterance: Peripheral pulses are strong and regular.
Back and spine have no deformities. Right horizontal gaze noted.
Glucose level is 137.
Current blood pressure is 176/80.
EKG shows sinus rhythm. No acute changes.
Oxygen saturation is 100% at this time.
Repeating 4-lead EKG. Still in sinus rhythm.
Performing 12-lead EKG now. Shows nonspecific ST/T wave changes.
Using Stroke/TIA protocol due to altered mental status and fall.
Glucose reading is unavailable now.
Blood pressure is now 187/115.
Oxygen saturation remains at 99%.
Current pulse is 80, regular.
Redocumenting 4-lead. Still in sinus.
12-lead is consistent with prior. Still shows non-specific wave changes.
The patient is reporting no pain.
Transporting to a Stroke Center per protocol.
Preparing on stretcher for transport.
Applying 5-point harness for secure movement in ambulance.
She is stable for transport at this time.
Transport destination is St. Mary's Hospital.
Stretcher secured in ambulance. Ready for en route vitals and reporting.
93-year-old female with altered mental status post-fall. GCS 11. Vital signs trending with elevated BP.
EKG shows nonspecific ST changes. Altered mental status consistent with possible stroke.
Right horizontal gaze noted. No arm drift or facial droop, but decreased grip strength reported.
Advised receiving ED of possible stroke. Performed 12-lead EKG with nonspecific findings.
Patient is alert but not oriented. Skin is warm, pink and dry. No signs of stroke at this time.
No active stroke signs presented - no motor drift, facial droop. Decreased grip strength and altered mental status documented.
No pain reported. Patient is oriented to self but not to person or place. Meds on board and documented.
She is being transferred onto bed now by sheet lift.
All equipment secured and patient stable for physician transfer.
Handing off latest EKG readings along with vitals and subjective report.
